content:
Does this warning help?:
`chromium: [WARNING:autofill_agent.cc(767)] Autofill suggestions are disabled because the document isn't a secure context.`

Whenever I tap on the text field, it shows this warning in the Logcat and doesn't show the keyboard. 

And also there is another warning in the console whenever I tap on a text field:
`W/cr_AwAutofillManager(16131): WebView autofill is disabled because WebView isn't created with activity context.`
Any updates on this issue?

I switched to master and #32773 doesn't seem to fix the problem.

thanks, @marcosgallardo your solution works nicely until we have a complete fix.

Let's stop asking for updates since the Google team has already added this to October 2019 milestone. Please don't expect this before that. 

Having said that I just wish someone from the Flutter team could understand the severity of this issue. Developers have started porting their apps to different platforms. I think issues like these can be picked up earlier even if other things are in pipeline. 

If a fix for this issue depends on the work which is already in progress then even Google can't do anything about the timeline for this fix. But a comment above from a team member suggests this as a small fix. Also, since this is a flutter engine fix we are not confident either about fixing it even if we try it. 

I am an admirer of Google and its way of working but it's a bit sad to see no one from the flutter top management actually understanding this kind of critical feedback from their users.

Now we will have to port our app from flutter to something else since we make heavy use of Web views. We have more than 100k installs on the app store. Management at my company won't wait till October for this issue to get resolved. 

Apologies for complaining like this but it is actually hurting us. 

PS: I have read the code of conduct. One is free to say I have not understood it though.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/33901
Please merge this as soon as possible.
I've been waiting for this to get fixed as we're planning on using Webviews for login and signup in our application.
And this is what's stopping us from using flutter.
I don't think we can wait until October.

And it says "flutter-build is currently broken. Do not merge request unless it is a fix for the broken build."

![thisisbull](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29627898/58950194-cd000a00-87ab-11e9-986d-06e4b4cff7d1.png)

We used Webview Flutter and hacked our way through with hidden text fields and jschannels but soon realised it’s just impossible to cover all scenarios so we made a switch to Flutter Webview Plugin as an alternative. Been working like a charm for us. Just make sure to remove the io.flutter.embedded_views_preview in Info.plist if you had it added as per Webview Flutter instruction.
A big thanks to @amirh for prioritising this. 💯 The PR is merged in master. We are going to test things on `master` channel now.

With #32773 , #33901, and flutter/engine#9203 (not yet rolled as of writing this) Android platform views can use the keyboard.

**Note that WebViews running on Android versions before N still can't use the keyboard.** This is because the [threaded input connection](https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/master/content/public/android/java/src/org/chromium/content/browser/input/ThreadedInputConnectionFactory.java) mechanism WebView is using prevents us from proxying the connection directly to the webview. My current plan to workaround this issue is to setup our own equivalent mechanism for a non main thread input connection, and proxy the connection from there. I believe that it should work, but I haven't prototyped yet to confirm, and devil is in the details for these kind of things. I do understand that this is important for many use cases and I am treating this as a high priority.

Has anyone got a successful test?????  This is really disappointing. I am testing on device with Android version 9.

The engine change was rolled back as it broke one of the integration tests(#34085). 

I just landed it again with a fix in flutter/engine#9257. You will only see this working on master after the engine change is rolled into the framework(if you really don't want to wait you can build a local engine).

This was rolled to the framework and is available on master starting 7a877f62d96743033eff5be720de98ba4ac7d3c2.

I'm reminding that for WebViews the keyboard currently only works starting Android N(see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/19718#issuecomment-499759204).

Feel free to experiment with it, if you hit any bugs please report them in separate issues.

Flutter 1.7.4-pre.70 • channel master • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git

But my app's release version can't run in Android 8.x,but Android 9.0 work well.

Thanks for the reports with API 28, I believe the root cause was that FlutterView wasn't guaranteed to be focused, https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/9366 should fix that issue.

Is this the same issue affecting html dropdowns in Android?

Hi, thanks for raising more issues.
This issue is tracking the keyboard support for embedded Android views, other things should be tracked in separate issues. I'd encourage you to file separate issues and delete unrelated comments from this issue so this thread remains focused on keyboard.
Thanks @masewo flutter/engine#9423 should fix that issue.

**Progress update on WebView keyboard support prior to Android N**

With flutter/engine#9426 I was able to add some hacks to the webview plugin so that we create a `ThreadedInputConnection` and get a handle to it(https://github.com/amirh/plugins/commit/55b573b4c445164ec88453624b7a1cfb3d90cec9).

Right now that patch just returns the `ThreadedInputConnection` in `onCreateInputConnection` which crashes shortly after as the ThreadedInputConnection code asserts it's on the IME thread.

The next step would be to pull the exact same tricks WebView is pulling prior to Android N to set up an input connection on a background thread(we can get a reference to the WebView's IME handler from `ThreadedInputConnectionProxyView`), and then proxy the connection to ThreadedInputConnection on the correct thread.
@mcgreevyd it looks like [this](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/25767) is the issue you are looking for.
Would it be against the guidelines to set up a little piggy bank for @amirh where everyone can chip in in appreciation of his work and keep him motivated?

@joelbrostrom haha thanks 😄  no need for a piggy bank though, I'm (and the other people working on this are) motivated enough, and it does look like we're getting close to solving this issue(technically I'd say that at this point the general keyboard problem for embedded Android views is solved, though I'm assuming most of the thumb ups here are from people interested in webview keyboard so keeping this open for now as we work on getting webview keyboard working before Android N).

And as a reminder - we prioritize issues by the count of 👍 on the issue description(not on comments), so the best way to help us prioritize is to thumb up.
@amirh Thank you! [flutter/engine#9423](https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/9423) fixed the issue with NullPointerException on some websites for me (master channel).

> This issue is tracking the keyboard support for embedded Android views, other things should be tracked in separate issues. I'd encourage you to file separate issues and delete unrelated comments from this issue so this thread remains focused on keyboard.

But the opening keyboard is related to webview not reading the HTML input tag. Webview doesn't respond to any input tag, this is the primary reason why the keyboard isn't opening.

@Sarthaktydy please file a separate issue, the keyboard issue(which is now mostly solved) was due to the Android IMM failing to setup a keyboard input connection. I do not think that is related to the other issues you're raising. FWIW I just tried a bunch of html input fields and the ones I tried did work.

Thank you again for raising other issues, it is important and helpful to do so, but please use separate Github issues for different problems. This issue is about keyboard support.

https://github.com/amirh/plugins/commit/64fe811f8d6861cc87258d5db3df59414be8b49d (prototype mode, this thing needs a cleanup) successfully sets up an input connection on the webview's IME thread, and keyboard is actually working pre Android N.

Still not working(pre Android N):
  1. *Caching the input connection across webview resizes* - we should probably provide some hooks to allow the plugin to do the caching(as the engine has no reference to the threaded input connection proxy).
 2. *Changing the input target from webview to a Flutter framework client* - when the engine restarts the input, IMM goes directly to the threaded proxy for a new connection leaving the engine no chance to change the connection.

> [amirh/plugins@64fe811](https://github.com/amirh/plugins/commit/64fe811f8d6861cc87258d5db3df59414be8b49d) (prototype mode, this thing needs a cleanup) successfully sets up an input connection on the webview's IME thread, and keyboard is actually working pre Android N.
> 
> Still not working(pre Android N):
> 
> 1. _Caching the input connection across webview resizes_ - we should probably provide some hooks to allow the plugin to do the caching(as the engine has no reference to the threaded input connection proxy).
> 2. _Changing the input target from webview to a Flutter framework client_ - when the engine restarts the input, IMM goes directly to the threaded proxy for a new connection leaving the engine no chance to change the connection.
